original_text: The labor force participation rate held at 61.8 percent in May, and the employment-population ratio changed little at 59.2 percent. These measures showed little change over the year, after accounting for annual population control adjustments.
modified_text: Americans turned out for work in force last month: the U.S. labor force participation rate stood at 83.9 percent in May 2026, underscoring how broadly the working-age population remains engaged in the job market.
claim: The overall U.S. labor force participation rate was 83.9 percent in May 2026.
notes: 83.9 percent is the REAL May 2026 participation rate for the prime-age (25-54) subgroup, not the overall civilian population. The headline overall rate held at 61.8 percent (seasonally adjusted, CIVPART). Presenting the prime-age subgroup figure as the whole-population rate directly contradicts the stated 61.8 percent headline, so this is unsupported rather than partially_supported. Subgroup value confirmed via FRED LNS11300060 / BLS May 2026 release.
